forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Replace connect-slot with pure per-session tool instancing (connecting)
+---
+## What
+
+Replace the mutable connect-slot event wiring (shipped in `feat(operations): add standard progress/completion event vocabulary…`) with **pure per-session tool instancing**. The shipped design holds one mutable sink per tool instance (`OperationEventSinkHolder`), so two sessions sharing a tool instance (e.g. a Router session and its fork) steal event delivery from each other — last connect wins. The fix: the capability protocol becomes a pure function returning a sink-bound *copy* of the tool; underlying reference-typed state stays shared, only the event route is per-copy.
+
+Rework in this package:
+- `EventEmittingTool`: replace `func connect(_ sink:)` (mutation) with `func connecting(_ sink: any OperationEventSink) -> any Tool` (pure copy; the copy shares the receiver's underlying state, only the event route differs). Keep the "hosts instantiate, users don't" contract in the docs: a host maps its `[any Tool]` list through `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connecting(sink) ?? tool` during session setup; conformance remains discoverable from an `any Tool` existential (no associated types on the protocol).
+- `EventEmittingContext`: replace the `OperationEventSinkHolder` requirement with a plain immutable value: `var operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)? { get }` plus `func connecting(_ sink:) -> Self` returning a copy with the sink replaced. Contexts are value types; reference-typed state inside them (actors etc.) is copied by reference and stays shared — document that this is the point.
+- **Delete `OperationEventSinkHolder`** (the lock, the `@unchecked Sendable`, the replace semantics — all gone; nothing is mutated after construction).
+- `OperationTool` conditional conformance: `extension OperationTool: EventEmittingTool where Context: EventEmittingContext` implements `connecting` as a copy with `context.connecting(sink)`. `OperationTool.init` throws (schema fusion) — provide a non-throwing internal copy path that reuses the already-fused schema rather than re-running the throwing initializer.
+- Operations that start long-running work capture `context.operationEventSink` **once, at operation start**, into whatever outlives the call (e.g. a detached supervisor) — document this capture-at-start rule on `EventEmittingContext`; it is what makes ownership stable across later re-instancing.
+- `OperationEvent` / `OperationEventSink` are unchanged.
+
+Update `EventEmittingToolTests` accordingly: the mixed-list discovery test becomes cast-and-`connecting` (asserting the returned copy posts to the given sink and the original still posts nowhere / to its own sink); the copy-shares-state test replaces the holder-reference test (two `connecting` copies over one shared reference-typed fixture state observe each other's state mutations while posting to different sinks); delete holder-specific tests.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `OperationEventSinkHolder` no longer exists; no public API mutates event wiring after construction
+- [ ] `tool.connecting(sinkA)` and `tool.connecting(sinkB)` post to their own sinks independently; the original posts into the void; all three share underlying reference-typed context state (one test proves state sharing + route independence simultaneously)
+- [ ] Discovery still works from an existential: `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)` succeeds for an opted-in fused tool in a mixed `[any Tool]` list, and the mapped result is usable as a session tool list
+- [ ] `connecting` does not re-run schema fusion (non-throwing, reuses the fused schema)
+- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate, including the capture-at-start rule and "hosts instantiate, users don't"
+- [ ] `swift test` fully green
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Reworked `Tests/OperationsTests/EventEmittingToolTests.swift` covering the criteria above
+- [ ] `swift test` fully green in this package
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,33 +0,0 @@
----
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: '80'
-title: Replace connect-slot with pure per-session tool instancing (connecting)
----
-## What
-
-Replace the mutable connect-slot event wiring (shipped in `feat(operations): add standard progress/completion event vocabulary…`) with **pure per-session tool instancing**. The shipped design holds one mutable sink per tool instance (`OperationEventSinkHolder`), so two sessions sharing a tool instance (e.g. a Router session and its fork) steal event delivery from each other — last connect wins. The fix: the capability protocol becomes a pure function returning a sink-bound *copy* of the tool; underlying reference-typed state stays shared, only the event route is per-copy.
-
-Rework in this package:
-- `EventEmittingTool`: replace `func connect(_ sink:)` (mutation) with `func connecting(_ sink: any OperationEventSink) -> any Tool` (pure copy; the copy shares the receiver's underlying state, only the event route differs). Keep the "hosts instantiate, users don't" contract in the docs: a host maps its `[any Tool]` list through `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connecting(sink) ?? tool` during session setup; conformance remains discoverable from an `any Tool` existential (no associated types on the protocol).
-- `EventEmittingContext`: replace the `OperationEventSinkHolder` requirement with a plain immutable value: `var operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)? { get }` plus `func connecting(_ sink:) -> Self` returning a copy with the sink replaced. Contexts are value types; reference-typed state inside them (actors etc.) is copied by reference and stays shared — document that this is the point.
-- **Delete `OperationEventSinkHolder`** (the lock, the `@unchecked Sendable`, the replace semantics — all gone; nothing is mutated after construction).
-- `OperationTool` conditional conformance: `extension OperationTool: EventEmittingTool where Context: EventEmittingContext` implements `connecting` as a copy with `context.connecting(sink)`. `OperationTool.init` throws (schema fusion) — provide a non-throwing internal copy path that reuses the already-fused schema rather than re-running the throwing initializer.
-- Operations that start long-running work capture `context.operationEventSink` **once, at operation start**, into whatever outlives the call (e.g. a detached supervisor) — document this capture-at-start rule on `EventEmittingContext`; it is what makes ownership stable across later re-instancing.
-- `OperationEvent` / `OperationEventSink` are unchanged.
-
-Update `EventEmittingToolTests` accordingly: the mixed-list discovery test becomes cast-and-`connecting` (asserting the returned copy posts to the given sink and the original still posts nowhere / to its own sink); the copy-shares-state test replaces the holder-reference test (two `connecting` copies over one shared reference-typed fixture state observe each other's state mutations while posting to different sinks); delete holder-specific tests.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `OperationEventSinkHolder` no longer exists; no public API mutates event wiring after construction
-- [ ] `tool.connecting(sinkA)` and `tool.connecting(sinkB)` post to their own sinks independently; the original posts into the void; all three share underlying reference-typed context state (one test proves state sharing + route independence simultaneously)
-- [ ] Discovery still works from an existential: `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)` succeeds for an opted-in fused tool in a mixed `[any Tool]` list, and the mapped result is usable as a session tool list
-- [ ] `connecting` does not re-run schema fusion (non-throwing, reuses the fused schema)
-- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate, including the capture-at-start rule and "hosts instantiate, users don't"
-- [ ] `swift test` fully green
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Reworked `Tests/OperationsTests/EventEmittingToolTests.swift` covering the criteria above
-- [ ] `swift test` fully green in this package
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,33 +1,40 @@
 ---
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '80'
-title: Replace connect-slot with pure per-session tool instancing (connecting)
+title: 'Two pure tool capabilities: EventEmittingTool.connecting + ForkableTool.forked (replace connect slot)'
 ---
 ## What

-Replace the mutable connect-slot event wiring (shipped in `feat(operations): add standard progress/completion event vocabulary…`) with **pure per-session tool instancing**. The shipped design holds one mutable sink per tool instance (`OperationEventSinkHolder`), so two sessions sharing a tool instance (e.g. a Router session and its fork) steal event delivery from each other — last connect wins. The fix: the capability protocol becomes a pure function returning a sink-bound *copy* of the tool; underlying reference-typed state stays shared, only the event route is per-copy.
+Replace the mutable connect-slot event wiring (shipped in `feat(operations): add standard progress/completion event vocabulary…`) with **two small orthogonal, pure capability protocols**. The shipped design holds one mutable sink per tool instance (`OperationEventSinkHolder`), so two sessions sharing a tool instance (e.g. a Router session and its fork) steal event delivery from each other — last connect wins. The replacement: nothing is mutated after construction; hosts derive per-session tool instances by pure functions.

-Rework in this package:
-- `EventEmittingTool`: replace `func connect(_ sink:)` (mutation) with `func connecting(_ sink: any OperationEventSink) -> any Tool` (pure copy; the copy shares the receiver's underlying state, only the event route differs). Keep the "hosts instantiate, users don't" contract in the docs: a host maps its `[any Tool]` list through `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connecting(sink) ?? tool` during session setup; conformance remains discoverable from an `any Tool` existential (no associated types on the protocol).
-- `EventEmittingContext`: replace the `OperationEventSinkHolder` requirement with a plain immutable value: `var operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)? { get }` plus `func connecting(_ sink:) -> Self` returning a copy with the sink replaced. Contexts are value types; reference-typed state inside them (actors etc.) is copied by reference and stays shared — document that this is the point.
-- **Delete `OperationEventSinkHolder`** (the lock, the `@unchecked Sendable`, the replace semantics — all gone; nothing is mutated after construction).
-- `OperationTool` conditional conformance: `extension OperationTool: EventEmittingTool where Context: EventEmittingContext` implements `connecting` as a copy with `context.connecting(sink)`. `OperationTool.init` throws (schema fusion) — provide a non-throwing internal copy path that reuses the already-fused schema rather than re-running the throwing initializer.
-- Operations that start long-running work capture `context.operationEventSink` **once, at operation start**, into whatever outlives the call (e.g. a detached supervisor) — document this capture-at-start rule on `EventEmittingContext`; it is what makes ownership stable across later re-instancing.
-- `OperationEvent` / `OperationEventSink` are unchanged.
+Land in this package:

-Update `EventEmittingToolTests` accordingly: the mixed-list discovery test becomes cast-and-`connecting` (asserting the returned copy posts to the given sink and the original still posts nowhere / to its own sink); the copy-shares-state test replaces the holder-reference test (two `connecting` copies over one shared reference-typed fixture state observe each other's state mutations while posting to different sinks); delete holder-specific tests.
+**1. `EventEmittingTool` reworked to pure instancing:**
+- Replace `func connect(_ sink:)` (mutation) with `func connecting(_ sink: any OperationEventSink) -> any Tool` (pure copy; the copy shares the receiver's underlying reference-typed state, only the event route differs). Docs keep the "hosts instantiate, users don't" contract: a host maps its `[any Tool]` list through `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)?.connecting(sink) ?? tool` at session setup; conformance stays discoverable from an `any Tool` existential (no associated types).
+- `EventEmittingContext`: replace the holder requirement with a plain immutable value — `var operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)? { get }` plus `func connecting(_ sink:) -> Self` (copy with sink replaced). Document the capture-at-start rule: operations that start long-running work capture the context's sink once, at operation start, into whatever outlives the call — that is what keeps event ownership with the session whose turn started the operation.
+- **Delete `OperationEventSinkHolder`** (lock, `@unchecked Sendable`, replace semantics — all gone).

+**2. New `ForkableTool` with a blanket value-semantics default:**
+- `public protocol ForkableTool: Tool { func forked() -> any Tool }` — a child session's instance of this tool, derived at fork time. Blanket default in a protocol extension: `func forked() -> any Tool { self }` — for a struct tool this is a genuine new instance (copy); document the caveat that the default assumes value semantics and a class-based tool must override.
+- `public protocol ForkableContext: Sendable { func forked() -> Self }` with the same blanket copy default. `OperationTool` (a struct) conforms to `ForkableTool` unconditionally: `forked()` returns a copy whose context is `(context as? any ForkableContext)?.forked() ?? context` — so fork semantics are context-owned and tool-internal; hosts never see the difference.
+- Host composition contract (document, host implements): at fork, `((tool as? any ForkableTool)?.forked() ?? tool)` then bind events via `connecting` if emitting. Plain tools conform to neither and pass through shared.
+
+**Shared plumbing:** `OperationTool.init` throws (schema fusion) — provide one non-throwing internal copy path (reusing the already-fused schema) that both `connecting` and `forked` use.
+
+`OperationEvent` / `OperationEventSink` are unchanged. Rework `EventEmittingToolTests` (and add `ForkableToolTests`): route independence, state sharing across copies, blanket-default fork behavior, mixed-list host mapping; delete holder-specific tests.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `OperationEventSinkHolder` no longer exists; no public API mutates event wiring after construction
-- [ ] `tool.connecting(sinkA)` and `tool.connecting(sinkB)` post to their own sinks independently; the original posts into the void; all three share underlying reference-typed context state (one test proves state sharing + route independence simultaneously)
-- [ ] Discovery still works from an existential: `(tool as? any EventEmittingTool)` succeeds for an opted-in fused tool in a mixed `[any Tool]` list, and the mapped result is usable as a session tool list
-- [ ] `connecting` does not re-run schema fusion (non-throwing, reuses the fused schema)
-- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate, including the capture-at-start rule and "hosts instantiate, users don't"
+- [ ] `OperationEventSinkHolder` no longer exists; no public API mutates tool or event wiring after construction
+- [ ] `tool.connecting(sinkA)` and `tool.connecting(sinkB)` post to their own sinks independently; the original posts into the void; all three share underlying reference-typed context state (one test proves state sharing + route independence together)
+- [ ] A fused `OperationTool` satisfies `tool as? any ForkableTool`; the blanket `forked()` returns a copy sharing reference-typed context state; a `ForkableContext`-conforming context's custom `forked()` is consulted (fixture test with a context that marks its forks)
+- [ ] Host mapping over a mixed `[any Tool]` list works for both protocols: non-conforming tools pass through identically (`===`-equivalent behavior), conforming ones yield independent copies
+- [ ] Neither `connecting` nor `forked` re-runs schema fusion (non-throwing shared copy path)
+- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate: capture-at-start rule, "hosts instantiate, users don't", the value-semantics caveat on the blanket `forked()` default, and the fork-then-connect composition order
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Reworked `Tests/OperationsTests/EventEmittingToolTests.swift` covering the criteria above
+- [ ] Reworked `Tests/OperationsTests/EventEmittingToolTests.swift` + new `ForkableToolTests.swift` covering the criteria above
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green in this package

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #long-running
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

Blanket default in a protocol extension: `func forked() -> any Tool { self }` — for a struct tool this is a genuine new instance (copy); document the caveat that the default assumes value semantics and a class-based tool must override.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,6 +4,29 @@
   id: 01ky7d8dyyzgv7b7hq4fw2y1yq
   text: '/finish scoped-batch: picked up this task (only ready task on the board). Starting implement→test→commit→review loop.'
   timestamp: 2026-07-23T11:55:00.446589+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky7e7zwk1dgkgnngm008ywsp
+  text: |-
+    Implemented via TDD per the /tdd workflow note.
+
+    What changed:
+    - Sources/Operations/EventEmittingTool.swift: replaced mutable `connect(_:)` with pure `connecting(_ sink:) -> any Tool`.
+    - Sources/Operations/EventEmittingContext.swift: replaced the holder requirement with a plain immutable `var operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)?` plus `func connecting(_ sink:) -> Self`.
+    - Deleted Sources/Operations/OperationEventSinkHolder.swift entirely (lock, @unchecked Sendable, replace semantics all gone).
+    - New Sources/Operations/ForkableTool.swift: `public protocol ForkableTool: Tool { func forked() -> any Tool }` with blanket `{ self }` default in a protocol extension, documenting the value-semantics caveat and the fork-then-connect host composition order.
+    - New Sources/Operations/ForkableContext.swift: `public protocol ForkableContext: Sendable { func forked() -> Self }` with the same blanket copy default.
+    - Sources/Operations/OperationTool.swift: added a private non-throwing init + `copy(context:)` helper reusing the already-fused `parameters` schema, shared by both `connecting(_:)` and `forked()` (neither re-runs SchemaFusion.fuse). `OperationTool` conforms to `ForkableTool` unconditionally; `forked()` returns a copy whose context is `(context as? any ForkableContext)?.forked() as? Context ?? context`.
+
+    Tests (TDD, watched RED before implementing — confirmed via `git stash` of the source changes, which produced the expected "cannot find type/member" compile errors, then restored and turned GREEN):
+    - Reworked Tests/OperationsTests/EventEmittingToolTests.swift: deleted all `OperationEventSinkHolder`-specific tests; added `connectingTwoSinksPostIndependentlyWhileSharingUnderlyingContextState` (route independence + shared reference-typed context state, via a `SharedRunLog` actor, in one test) and reworked the mixed `[any Tool]` list test to map through `connecting(_:)` instead of mutating `.connect(_:)`.
+    - New Tests/OperationsTests/ForkableToolTests.swift: blanket-default behavior (a bare `Tool` conforming to `ForkableTool` without overriding `forked()`), `OperationTool`'s unconditional `ForkableTool` conformance, the no-`ForkableContext` fallback still sharing reference-typed state (a `SharedCounter` actor), a `ForkableContext`-conforming fixture that marks its forks (increments a `generation` field) being consulted, and mixed `[any Tool]` list host mapping for `forked()`.
+
+    Verification: `swift build` and `swift test` both green — 184 tests total (84 OperationsTests + 34 OperationsMacrosTests + 44 OperationsCLITests + 22 NotesToolTests), 0 failures, 0 warnings. DocCoverageTests specifically re-verified green (every new public declaration documented).
+
+    Self-administered adversarial double-check (no Task-tool subagent dispatch available in this sandboxed environment, so I ran the double-check process directly): caught one real issue — `ForkableToolTests.fusedOperationToolSatisfiesForkableToolConformance` cast the concrete `OperationTool<MarkingForkContext>` directly to `any ForkableTool`, which the compiler statically knows always succeeds (unconditional conformance) and warns about. Fixed by erasing to `any Tool` first (matching how a real host actually discovers the conformance), re-verified zero warnings after the fix.
+
+    All acceptance criteria checked off against the task description. Leaving in `doing` per /implement workflow — ready for /review.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-23T12:12:14.611957+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: 'Two pure tool capabilities: EventEmittingTool.connecting + ForkableTool.forked (replace connect slot)'
